summary: Backend regression suite executed after config-only revert of /etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf (removed the previously-added --tunnel flag from the Expo start command). Full 51-test suite (33 iteration_8 email-OTP + 18 iteration_9 today/tomorrow) PASSED 100% in 189.99s. Preview URL is back to healthy: GET https://repo-preview-86.preview.emergentagent.com/api/ → 200 {app:'MessMate', status:'ok'}; GET / → 200 (Metro serving); localhost:3000 → 200 (Metro process up, no more --tunnel/@expo/ngrok crash). Supervisor status: backend RUNNING, expo RUNNING, mongodb RUNNING. Confirmed the Cloudflare 502 root cause is fully resolved and no functional regression was introduced by the revert.

minor.issue: Passlib DeprecationWarning: `crypt` module deprecated in Python 3.13. Cosmetic only; bcrypt hashing works and all 51 tests pass. Pre-existing, unrelated to this revert.

critical_code_review_comments: /etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf line 10 now reads `exec yarn expo start --port 3000` — the `--tunnel` flag has been correctly removed. Metro binds to :3000 as expected and the K8s ingress serves the preview URL without upstream errors.
Backend server.py is unchanged from iteration_9/10 (still 1968 lines — the >700-line guideline flag from prior iterations still stands but is out of scope here).
seed_data_creation: None. Reused existing self-cleaning TEST_-prefixed suites.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 is a config-only revert verification. --tunnel flag removed from Expo supervisor command (was breaking startup because @expo/ngrok is not installed → Metro never bound to :3000 → K8s ingress returned Cloudflare 502 upstream). Now: supervisord starts `yarn expo start --port 3000` cleanly, Metro listens on 3000, preview URL returns 200 for both / and /api/. All 51 backend regression tests green. SMTP is live (Gmail App Password); each register test sends a real email — consider OTP_DEV_MODE for CI. Public URL env var is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env.
rca_of_issue: Original 502: previous config change added `--tunnel` to the Expo start command, but @expo/ngrok is not installed in this environment, so `yarn expo start --port 3000 --tunnel` exited non-zero → supervisor kept restarting expo → port 3000 never had a listener → K8s ingress upstream failed → Cloudflare returned 502. Mitigation: revert removed `--tunnel`. Verified fix: supervisorctl shows expo RUNNING for >1 minute, localhost:3000 = 200, public URL / and /api/ both = 200, full backend suite still 51/51 green. No code changes were needed — this was purely an infra/config regression fix.
